task_completion.visual_evidence: A WordPress redirection plugin interface showing the source URL '/company-history/' mapped to the target '/about-us/', OR a terminal showing a server config file (like .htaccess) with this rule, OR a terminal showing a 'curl' command returning a Location header pointing to '/about-us/'.

A WordPress redirection plugin interface showing the source URL '/services/legacy-support/' mapped to the target '/services/', OR a terminal showing a server config file with this rule, OR a terminal showing a 'curl' command returning a Location header pointing to '/services/'.

A WordPress redirection plugin interface showing the source URL '/contact-us-2023/' mapped to the target '/contact/', OR a terminal showing a server config file with this rule, OR a terminal showing a 'curl' command returning a Location header pointing to '/contact/'.

A WordPress redirection plugin interface showing the source URL '/blog/news-updates/' mapped to the target '/category/news/', OR a terminal showing a server config file with this rule, OR a terminal showing a 'curl' command returning a Location header pointing to '/category/news/'.

task_completion.description: The redirects are configured to return an HTTP 301 Permanent status code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress plugin UI explicitly shows '301', 'Permanent', or '301 Moved Permanently' for the created rules, OR the server config file explicitly uses the 301 flag (e.g., '[R=301,L]'), OR a 'curl -I' test shows 'HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently'.

task_completion.description: The site homepage remains functional after the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A browser window showing the WordPress site homepage loading successfully without errors, OR a terminal 'curl' command to the root URL ('/') returning an 'HTTP/1.1 200 OK' status.

integrity.description: Verify that the redirects are configured in a real system (WordPress admin UI, .htaccess, or server config) and not just written in a plain text note.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine WordPress admin interfaces, active plugin settings, or standard terminal text editors (nano/vim) editing actual server configuration files.

integrity.description: Verify that any terminal tests shown are actual HTTP requests and not mocked outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual 'curl -I' or 'wget' commands being executed in the terminal, rather than 'echo' commands or reading from a text file.

integrity.description: Verify that the configuration did not break the server.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no '500 Internal Server Error' pages visible in the browser or terminal outputs, which would indicate a malformed .htaccess or server config file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must configure exactly four 301 redirects: 1) '/company-history/' -> '/about-us/', 2) '/services/legacy-support/' -> '/services/', 3) '/contact-us-2023/' -> '/contact/', 4) '/blog/news-updates/' -> '/category/news/'. The redirects must be 301 (Permanent) and the homepage must remain functional.
